methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous transportation company founded by Raquel Urtasun, operates within a Transportation sector peer set of 7 private companies including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Slate Auto, and ZeroAvia. No public comparables exist in the immediate competitive window.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable approach given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peer density in Transportation (fewer than 3 datapoints meeting narrow filters). Using Waabi's $1.0B total capital raised as a proxy for Series C entry value, a 1.7x step-up multiplier—typical for progression to the subsequent funding round—yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. This multiplier reflects expected investor returns and dilution patterns observed across later-stage venture rounds.

The triangulated range supports a median valuation of $1.7B, with a low-to-high band spanning $1.4B to $2.0B. Given reliance on a single methodology, key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for this specific sector and stage, and the company's actual progress metrics since the $1.0B fundraise. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient comparable data at matching stage and valuation tier. Materiality of subsequent financing activity and market conditions in autonomous transportation would inform tightening of this range.
